prompt: A small team is choosing between writing software in TypeScript or Python for a new internal data tool. Walk through how you would help them decide, in prose (not a bulleted list).
response:
To help the team decide between TypeScript and Python for their new internal data tool, I would start by carefully considering the key requirements and constraints of the project. Factors like the size and complexity of the tool, the types of data it will be processing, and how it will integrate with their existing infrastructure are all important to evaluate.

I would also want to understand the team's current skillset and experience. If they already have strong Python expertise and existing codebases in Python, there may be benefits to sticking with what they know to maintain productivity. On the other hand, if they are willing to invest in learning TypeScript, it could pay dividends in terms of type safety and scalability as the tool grows.

Next, I would research how each language compares on the critical dimensions for this project. For a data-focused application, Python has the advantage of having a large ecosystem of powerful data science and machine learning libraries. Its dynamic nature allows for rapid prototyping. TypeScript is a statically-typed superset of JavaScript, which enforces more rigorous type checking. This can help catch errors early and make the codebase easier to maintain and extend over time.

Other considerations include things like available support and resources, community size, future trends, deployment options, and performance benchmarks. I would dig into how each language ranks on these for the specific task at hand.

Taking all of this into account, I would work with the team to make a decision that balances the immediate needs with longer-term strategic goals. We would discuss the tradeoffs of each option and I would lean on my experience to guide them to a choice that sets the project up for success. After selecting a language, I would help craft a plan to upskill the team as needed and establish best practices to get the most out of our chosen technology.
